question_text: A sound wave has a frequency of 440 Hz. What does this tell us?

options.label: A
options.text: The wave travels 440 metres per second

options.label: B
options.text: The wave completes 440 complete cycles in one second

options.label: C
options.text: The wave has a wavelength of 440 metres

options.label: D
options.text: The wave reaches a maximum height of 440 metres

correct_answer: The wave completes 440 complete cycles in one second
explanation: Frequency is defined as the number of complete waves (or cycles) produced per second. It is measured in Hertz (Hz). Therefore, a frequency of 440 Hz means exactly 440 complete wave cycles occur every second. This is the standard definition taught in KS3 physics.
